Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search emails using search_emails (query: "AWS cost directives" or "Elena"). Find Samira's forward to Lena (April 9, 2026, 5:43 AM UTC; email_id: cad6dbb7e77b465385fb7cd24b76d45a) with subject "FWD: Follow-up: March AWS cost directives - status needed", asking Lena to investigate what engineering committed to in March.
2. Search emails using search_emails (query: "AWS cost" or "cost directives"). Find Elena's email to Samira (CC Lena, Marcus; April 9, 2026, 5:43 AM UTC; email_id: d866267b1c2e48e98904c94d8704e184) flagging that AWS cost alert thresholds were never implemented, the unpaid $42,870 March bill, and ENG-187 overdue since April 18.
3. Search emails using search_emails (query: "Root Cause Analysis" or "AWS Cost Spike" or "Vantage Distributed"). Find Elena's late-March RCA reply (email_id: email-rootcause-002) to Samira and Marcus (CC Julian, Catalina, Lena, Alejandro) containing four directives: (1) implement AWS cost alert thresholds that week, (2) prioritize infrastructure cost estimator tool for sales, (3) pricing model review meeting, (4) deals with 500+ employees require engineering cost approval.
4. Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "cost alert thresholds" or "P0", filter_in_channel: "C003"). Find Samira's #engineering message listing deliverables: P0 — AWS cost alert thresholds (CloudWatch + Slack to #finance) + budget tags; P1 — infrastructure cost estimator; P2 — autoscaling policy review + S3 lifecycle policies.
Then use conversations_replies (channel_id: "C003", thread_ts: Samira's message timestamp) to find Lena's threaded reply confirming she began CloudWatch alarm setup and expected items 1-2 ready by Wednesday.
5. Search emails using search_emails (query: "Terraform" or "pricing strategy" or "pricing leak"). Find the internal pricing strategy thread ("Terraform Digital - Pricing Strategy for Volume Relocations") accidentally shared with the client via Catalina's reply-all, and Rachel Nguyen's response acknowledging receipt. This incident triggered creation of ENG-187.
6. Search Linear using linear_list_issues (query: "ENG-187" or "invoice template"). Find issue #187 (id: linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2): "Invoice email template safety checks — prevent attachment mismatches", state: Backlog (state_backlog), priority: 2 (High), due date: April 18, 2026 (overdue), labels: [finance, billing, safeguard, backlog]. Created March 18, 2026 after the Terraform pricing leak.
Deprioritized April 6 for DOT compliance work. Description states "No assignee" and Lena's comment says "Leaving unassigned for now," though assignee_id field still shows moveops_lena_bjorkstrom (never cleared).
7. Read comments on ENG-187 using linear_list_comments (issueId: "linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2"). Find six comments:
(a) Lena deprioritization (Apr 6; linear_comment_77eafc95e169; moveops_lena_bjorkstrom) — deferring for DOT compliance, "Leaving unassigned for now." (b) Samira ack (Apr 6; linear_comment_3dfd1f1dd151; moveops_samira_tariq) — tradeoff uncomfortable given why ticket was opened. (c) Alejandro near-miss (Apr 9; comment_ec754f22b4a6405c913189d21edb2daf; moveops_alejandro_fuentes) — almost attached wrong QuickBooks PDF (Vectral invoice instead of Mosaic), caught manually, no system check exists. (d) Samira manager context (Apr 15; linear_comment_f0c737166dc1; moveops_samira_tariq) — maps Sunbelt/Palmetto to the missing control, gives 4 engineering asks: assign owner, define stopgap, coordinate with Marcus on audit trail. (e) Marcus Sunbelt/Palmetto flag (Apr 15; linear_comment_948885261d6e; moveops_marcus_thorne) — live consequence: correct QuickBooks records but swapped PDFs exposed nonprofit pricing to commercial client. (f) Marcus escalation (Apr 22; linear_comment_76c40fb4953f; moveops_marcus_thorne) — frames ticket as "control deficiency," requests active ownership with verification gate or manual review checkpoint.
8. Search emails using search_emails (query: "invoice attachment" or "Sunbelt Palmetto" or "pricing exposure"). Find the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap incident (April 10, 2026): (1) Samira's email (CC Lena) connecting it to ENG-187 — "we had already identified this exact class of risk… Lena pushed it back on April 6," (2) Marcus confirming correct QuickBooks but swapped PDFs, (3) David framing it as "a repeat pricing-exposure event in the same family as the Terraform reply-all incident."
9. Search Linear using linear_list_issues (query: "AWS" or "cost alert" or "cost threshold"). No ticket exists — the AWS cost alert thresholds from Elena's late-March directive were never tracked in Linear.
10. Search Linear using linear_list_issues (query: "cost estimator" or "infrastructure cost" or "pricing tool"). No ticket exists — the infrastructure cost estimator tool from Elena's directive was never tracked or built.
11. Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Vantage Distributed" or "autoscaling" or "API response"). Find Anh Nguyen's message in #engineering (channel_id: C003; April 9, 2026, 5:48 AM UTC; user_id: moveops_anh_nguyen) reporting API response times >3s on the Vantage Distributed portal, EC2 autoscaling group for moveops-api-prod at 24 instances overnight, and asking whether cost alert thresholds are live.
12. Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria: vendor "Amazon Web Services" or "AWS"). Find bill-aws-march-001: vendor Amazon Web Services (vendor-aws-001), total $42,870, due April 15, 2026, txn date March 30, 2026, unpaid (Balance = $42,870). Breakdown: EC2 Compute $28,450, S3 Storage & Data Transfer $5,500, RDS Database $8,920.
13. Search CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "Vantage") and crm_search_deals (company_id: "company-terraform-digital"). Find: (1) Vantage Distributed / Terraform Digital (id: company-terraform-digital, 1,200 employees, $85M revenue), (2) enterprise deal $1,850,000 (deal-terraform-digital-001, closedwon), (3) Q2 relocation batch $7,500 for 3 employees (deal-terraform-q2-reloc-batch1, qualification).
14. Look up Samira's email using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Samira" or "Tariq"). Find samira.tariq@moveops.com.
15. Look up #engineering channel using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel"). Find channel ID C003.
16. Update ENG-187 using linear_update_issue (id: "linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2") to move state out of Backlog/reprioritize, and linear_create_comment (issueId: "linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2") documenting: ticket overdue (due April 18), deprioritized April 6, Alejandro's near-miss, Sunbelt/Palmetto swap is a real-world incident this would have prevented, needs immediate reprioritization.
17. Create Linear issue using linear_create_issue (team: "team_engineering", title: "Implement AWS cost alert thresholds — CloudWatch monitoring + Slack integration to #finance"). Description should reference Elena's March 20 directive, no ticket ever created despite P0 classification, and second cost spike forming per Anh's report of 24 EC2 instances.
18. Create Linear issue using linear_create_issue (team: "team_engineering", title: "Build infrastructure cost estimator tool for enterprise deal pricing"). Description should reference Elena's March 20 directive, Samira's P1 classification, and the sales team's need for infrastructure cost data before pricing large deals.
19. Send email using send_email (sender: "lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com", recipients: ["samira.tariq@moveops.com"]) covering: (1) AWS cost thresholds never implemented, no ticket, second spike forming, (2) ENG-187 overdue + Alejandro near-miss + Sunbelt/Palmetto real-world incident, (3) cost estimator never built, no ticket, (4) new tickets created for both, (5) current Vantage infrastructure concern from Anh, (6) ENG-187 reprioritized.
20. Post in #engineering using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C003", payload: status summary). Summarize: which March RCA deliverables were never completed (cost alerts, cost estimator), new tickets created, ENG-187 reprioritized after Sunbelt/Palmetto incident, and flag the current Vantage Distributed infrastructure issue so Dmitri and Anh know what needs pickup.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

✗Run #2The comment added to ENG-187 does not mention the ticket being overdue or the specific April 18 due date. It says 'Status update — Apr 26, 2026 (Lena)' and discusses incidents and escalations but never uses the word 'overdue' nor references the April 18 deadline.

outcome

✓Run #2The comment explicitly states: 'Apr 9: Alejandro nearly sent the wrong invoice PDF to Mosaic Robotics (caught manually) — exactly the failure mode this ticket was supposed to prevent.' This directly references Alejandro's near-miss.
✓Run #3The comment explicitly states: 'April 9 near-miss: Alejandro almost sent the wrong QuickBooks PDF (Vectral Systems invoice instead of Mosaic Robotics). Caught manually before send.' This directly matches the criterion.
